conflicts: The grade's buy level (515) is below the current price (~592), so the deepest discount entry has already passed — chasing here gives worse risk/reward than waiting for a pullback.

plain_english: Every source agrees wheat is a patient buy down in its cheap zone, and there's no disagreement about direction. The problem is the best buy price (around 515) sits below where wheat trades now (about 592), so the ideal entry has already gone — and the newest note literally says 'accumulate patiently', meaning don't chase. Wait for a dip back into the cheap zone (roughly 480-560) to start buying; first upside targets are around 638-655, then 720-800 if it keeps climbing. The idea is wrong if price falls back below about 480.
